questions.question: A cubic polynomial $p(x) = x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6$ is represented graphically. The graph of $y = p(x)$ intersects the $x$-axis at three distinct points $A$, $B$, and $C$. (i) Find the coordinates of these points. (ii) If a new polynomial $q(x)$ is formed such that its zeros are the squares of the zeros of $p(x)$, determine $q(x)$ in terms of its coefficients. (iii) Based on the number of intersections with the $x$-axis, justify whether the graph of $q(x)$ will intersect the $x$-axis at more or fewer points than $p(x)$.
questions.answer: (i) $(1, 0), (2, 0), (3, 0)$; (ii) $q(x) = x^3 - 14x^2 + 49x - 36$; (iii) The graph of $q(x)$ will also intersect the $x$-axis at $3$ points.

steps: Find the zeros of $p(x)$ by factorizing $x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6 = 0$. Using the factor theorem, test $x=1$: $1 - 6 + 11 - 6 = 0$, so $(x-1)$ is a factor.
Perform polynomial division or synthetic division to find $p(x) = (x-1)(x^2 - 5x + 6) = (x-1)(x-2)(x-3)$. The zeros are $1, 2, 3$. The intersection points are $(1,0), (2,0), (3,0)$.
Let the zeros of $q(x)$ be $\alpha^2, \beta^2, \gamma^2$ where $\alpha=1, \beta=2, \gamma=3$. The new zeros are $1, 4, 9$.
Calculate the sum and product of the new zeros: Sum $= 1+4+9 = 14$; Sum of products taken two at a time $= 1(4) + 4(9) + 9(1) = 4 + 36 + 9 = 49$; Product $= 1(4)(9) = 36$.
Since $q(x)$ has $3$ distinct real zeros, its graph will intersect the $x$-axis at $3$ distinct points, which is the same number of points as $p(x)$.
